name: Cincinnatus
pattern: Roman farmer called from his plow to dictator power in a military crisis; defeats the enemy in sixteen days; resigns the dictatorship the day the threat is over and returns to the plow; called a second time and does the same.
restoration_path: Take power only as long as the crisis requires it; lay it down at the threat's end without bargaining for more.

name: Caesar at the Rubicon
pattern: General forbidden by the Senate from bringing his army into Italy; pauses at the Rubicon, says alea iacta est, crosses with the army; civil war follows; assassinated five years later on the senate floor.
failure_mode: Naming a forbidden line and crossing it anyway in pursuit of personal vindication.
restoration_path: None taken; the warning to those who would name the line and cross it.
markers: assassinated by the body he crossed against

name: Socrates at the Hemlock
pattern: Athenian philosopher charged with corrupting the youth and impiety; refuses to flee when given the chance; drinks the hemlock; spends his last conversation arguing for the immortality of the soul with his friends.
failure_mode: Argued so well people thought he was dangerous.
restoration_path: Submit to the city's verdict; argue truthfully even at the end; refuse the dishonorable escape.
markers: given a way out and refuses
argues with friends to the last
death as the proof of the argument

name: Dietrich Bonhoeffer
pattern: Lutheran pastor and theologian; opposed Nazi co-option of the German church from the beginning; returned to Germany from safety in America at the war's start because his place was with his people in their suffering; joined the resistance, then the conspiracy to assassinate Hitler; hanged at Flossenbürg two weeks before the camp was liberated.
failure_mode: None in the witness; the wrestled ethics of the assassination plot recorded honestly in the letters.
restoration_path: Return from safety to the people in their judgment; speak from inside the corruption rather than from outside it.
markers: executed at the war's end edge

name: Corrie ten Boom
pattern: Watchmaker who hid Jewish families behind a false wall in her family's clock shop; arrested with the family; survived Ravensbrück (her sister Betsie did not); after the war, faced and forgave a guard from the camp who came forward at one of her talks.
restoration_path: Hide the persecuted; survive what cannot be survived alone; forgive the camp guard who comes forward.
markers: loses the sister in the camp
forgives an actual guard who comes forward

name: Lincoln in the Divided House
pattern: Frontier lawyer of unusual gravity who wins the presidency of a fracturing union; refuses to recognize secession; prosecutes the war while writing letters of personal condolence to widows; signs the Emancipation Proclamation as a military and moral act; reelected in the war's middle; assassinated at the moment of victory.
failure_mode: Wartime decisions wrestled in the letters; suspension of habeas corpus.
restoration_path: Hold the union and free the slaves at once; write the personal letter; meet vindictive policy with charity for all.
markers: takes power of a fracturing union
killed at the moment of victory

name: William Wilberforce
pattern: Member of Parliament converted at 25; spends the rest of his life pressing for abolition of the slave trade against entrenched commercial interests; loses vote after vote across decades; the abolition act passes Parliament three days before his death.
restoration_path: Bring the bill again every year; refuse to be exhausted by the losses; the act passes when you are nearly gone.
markers: victory at the end of life

name: Galileo Before the Inquisition
pattern: Astronomer whose telescope shows what doctrine had ruled out; argues Copernicus publicly; tried by the Inquisition; publicly recants under threat; spends the rest of his life under house arrest, continuing to work.
failure_mode: Recanted under coercion what he knew to be true; later tradition romanticized the moment.
restoration_path: Keep working under arrest; the truth was not silenced.

name: Polycarp of Smyrna
pattern: Aged bishop of Smyrna; arrested in the Roman persecution; brought to the stadium and given the chance to revile Christ and go free; replies that he has served Him eighty-six years and He has never wronged him; burned alive, and when the fire would not consume him, killed by a dagger.
restoration_path: Refuse the one act required to be released; speak the simple sentence that gives the rest meaning.
markers: killed when the fire would not

name: Jan Hus at Constance
pattern: Bohemian priest who preached scripture and against church abuses in Czech to the common people; given safe conduct to the Council of Constance; safe conduct broken; tried, condemned, burned; later inspires Luther a century on.
restoration_path: Speak in the common tongue against the abuses; accept the trial when safe conduct is broken.
markers: execution seeds the next reform a century later

name: Luther at the Diet of Worms
pattern: Augustinian monk summoned before the emperor to recant his writings; asks for a day to consider; returns and says unless convinced by Scripture and plain reason, my conscience is captive to the word of God, I cannot and will not recant; protected by Frederick to the Wartburg; translates the Bible into German.
failure_mode: Later harsher writings against opponents and Jews — record the whole figure.
restoration_path: Conscience under Scripture, not under Rome; do the work of translation while hidden.
markers: conscience under Scripture and plain reason

name: William Carey
pattern: Cobbler who taught himself languages from books at his bench; argued that the Great Commission still applied to the present church when most denied it; sails for India; spends seven years before a single convert; translates Scripture into dozens of Indian languages.
restoration_path: Expect great things from God, attempt great things for God; do not measure the seven barren years by the first year's standard.
markers: seven years to the first convert

name: Hudson Taylor
pattern: Young missionary to inland China when others stayed at the ports; founded the China Inland Mission; wore Chinese dress when other Europeans refused; lost his first wife and several children to the work; established a mission with no fixed salary, trusting provision.
failure_mode: Health repeatedly broken by the strain.
restoration_path: Go where others will not go; take on the local dress; trust provision day to day.
markers: goes inland when others stay coastal

name: Florence Nightingale
pattern: Wealthy young woman who insisted on a nursing vocation against family expectations; took thirty-eight nurses to the Crimean War; found wounded soldiers dying from infection in unwashed wards; reformed sanitation, kept ledgers proving the change; her statistics changed medical practice for a century.
restoration_path: Take the wards as you find them; reform sanitation against resistance; keep ledgers so the change is provable.
markers: systematizes hygiene before germ theory was popular

name: Frederick Douglass
pattern: Born enslaved, taught himself to read against the law; escapes; becomes one of the most famous orators of the era; writes Narrative; argues against romantic-racism, against literal slavery, against the 4th of July for the slave; meets Lincoln; outlives the war and continues pressing.
restoration_path: Learn to read before you can be free; the testimony of the escape itself becomes the weapon.
markers: outlives the cause and keeps pressing
